failure_modes.text: Teams can label ordinary scale benefits as network effects without proving that each added participant improves value for the next one.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders label ordinary scale benefits as network effects
failure_modes.mitigation: Require proof of the feedback loop: show that adding one more participant measurably improves value for existing participants, not just that the product scales with volume.

failure_modes.text: Local success can be mistaken for critical mass before the threshold for self-sustaining adoption is actually real.
failure_modes.source_quote: Mistaking Local Success for Critical Mass
failure_modes.mitigation: Define the critical-mass threshold quantitatively and track adoption against it; do not declare network effects until the self-sustaining point is empirically reached.

failure_modes.text: Teams can overcomplicate the system before viability by trying to maximize every connection or data point too early.
failure_modes.mitigation: Defer complexity until the core loop works: resist adding features, integrations, or data layers until the minimal network-effect mechanism is validated with real users.

premortem_questions.text: What density is actually needed to reach the critical mass point in this market or segment?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What density ... is needed to reach the critical mass point

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe to accept that critical mass is not reachable here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would we have to believe to accept that critical mass is not reachable?

premortem_questions.text: Can the business model be tweaked so the threshold becomes reachable before organic growth is expected to carry it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Can you tweak the business model so that this critical mass point is reachable

heuristics.text: Separate threshold effects from ordinary growth by asking whether each added participant actually increases value for the next one.
heuristics.source_quote: value of adoption rises materially as more users ... join

heuristics.text: Use seeding intentionally when the network would otherwise fail before it reaches viable density.
heuristics.source_quote: seed each area with its own tools

heuristics.text: Focus on the few users, nodes, or features that matter most for pushing the system past critical mass.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on threshold mechanics, self-reinforcement, seeding, and anti-fake-network-effects discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broad platform-growth rhetoric because the raw source is stronger on critical mass and feedback logic.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses the source's Pareto-guided focus rule so the chunk stays compact.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on critical-mass, feedback-loops, systems-thinking, and pareto-principle because those models explain threshold crossing and self-reinforcement.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on cognitive-load-theory and confirmation-bias because both can distort adoption or critical-mass diagnosis.
structured_tensions: The source is clearest on ally pressure and anti-patterns, so structured tensions should stay conservative.
